segments.text: Meanwhile, Moses was shepherding the flock of his father-in-law Jethro, the priest of Midian. He led the flock to the far side of the wilderness and came to Horeb, the mountain of God.
There the angel of the LORD appeared to him in a blazing fire from within a bush. Moses saw the bush ablaze with fire, but it was not consumed.
So Moses thought,
I must go over and see this marvelous sight. Why is the bush not burning up?
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: When the LORD saw that he had gone over to look, God called out to him from within the bush,
Moses, Moses!
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Here I am,
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: he answered.
Do not come any closer,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: God said.
Take off your sandals, for the place where you are standing is holy ground.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Then He said,
I am the God of your father, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: At this, Moses hid his face, for he was afraid to look at God.
The LORD said,
I have indeed seen the affliction of My people in Egypt. I have heard them crying out because of their oppressors, and I am aware of their sufferings.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: I have come down to rescue them from the hand of the Egyptians and to bring them up out of that land to a good and spacious land, a land flowing with milk and honey—the home of the Canaanites, Hittites, Amorites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And now the cry of the Israelites has reached Me, and I have seen how severely the Egyptians are oppressing them.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Therefore, go! I am sending you to Pharaoh to bring My people the Israelites out of Egypt.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But Moses asked God,
Who am I, that I should go to Pharaoh and bring the Israelites out of Egypt?
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: I will surely be with you,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: God said,
and this will be the sign to you that I have sent you: When you have brought the people out of Egypt, all of you will worship God on this mountain.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Then Moses asked God,
Suppose I go to the Israelites and say to them, ‘The God of your fathers has sent me to you,’ and they ask me, ‘What is His name?’ What should I tell them?
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: God said to Moses,
I AM WHO I AM. This is what you are to say to the Israelites: ‘I AM has sent me to you.’
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: God also told Moses,

Say to the Israelites, ‘The LORD, the God of your fathers—the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob—has sent me to you.’ This is My name forever, and this is how I am to be remembered in every generation.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Go, assemble the elders of Israel and say to them, ‘The LORD, the God of your fathers—the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob—has appeared to me and said: I have surely attended to you and have seen what has been done to you in Egypt.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And I have promised to bring you up out of your affliction in Egypt, into the land of the Canaanites, Hittites, Amorites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites—a land flowing with milk and honey.’
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The elders of Israel will listen to what you say, and you must go with them to the king of Egypt and tell him, ‘The LORD, the God of the Hebrews, has met with us. Now please let us take a three-day journey into the wilderness, so that we may sacrifice to the LORD our God.’
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But I know that the king of Egypt will not allow you to go unless a mighty hand compels him.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: So I will stretch out My hand and strike the Egyptians with all the wonders I will perform among them. And after that, he will release you.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And I will grant this people such favor in the sight of the Egyptians that when you leave, you will not go away empty-handed.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Every woman shall ask her neighbor and any woman staying in her house for silver and gold jewelry and clothing, and you will put them on your sons and daughters. So you will plunder the Egyptians.
segments.speaker: God
